notes: Attempted reproduction from a macOS arm64 host using the reporter's SkiaSharp 3.119.1 + Svg.Skia 3.2.1 code path and a fallback test on 2.88.9. On both versions, SKDocument.CreateXps returned null and produced a 0-byte XPS file on macOS, so the Windows-specific XPS scaling behavior could not be observed. A Docker Linux attempt was also blocked because the local Docker daemon was unavailable.

versionResults.notes: Reporter-style repro project built and ran, but SKDocument.CreateXps returned null and output.xps was empty.
Legacy fallback repro also returned null from SKDocument.CreateXps with a 0-byte output.xps.
Cross-platform check blocked: docker run failed with daemon connection error.

reproductionSteps.description: Collect environment details from /tmp/repro-3400 (outside repo global.json constraints) and verify SDK/workloads/docker availability.
reproductionSteps.command: cd /tmp/repro-3400 && dotnet --info && dotnet workload list && docker --version && uname -a
reproductionSteps.output: dotnet SDK 10.0.102, host runtime 10.0.2, RID osx-arm64. Workloads include wasm-tools and mobile workloads. Docker CLI present: 28.1.1. Host OS: Darwin arm64.

reproductionSteps.description: Run reporter-version console repro using SVG with embedded image, then generate PDF and XPS via SKDocument APIs.
reproductionSteps.command: cd /tmp/repro-3400/reporter-host && dotnet new console -n ReproReporter --framework net9.0 && cd ReproReporter && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 3.119.1 && dotnet add package Svg.Skia --version 3.2.1 && dotnet build && dotnet run && ls -l output.pdf output.xps
reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded. Runtime output: FAIL: SKDocument.CreateXps returned null. output.pdf was created (207886 bytes); output.xps size was 0 bytes.

filesCreated.description: Reporter-style repro program loading an SVG with an embedded <image>, then writing PDF and XPS with identical scaling.
Console project targeting net9.0 with SkiaSharp 3.119.1 and Svg.Skia 3.2.1.

reproductionSteps.description: Attempt cross-platform Linux verification in Docker to rule out a non-Windows host artifact.
reproductionSteps.command: docker run --rm --platform linux/amd64 mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:9.0 bash -lc '<repro setup and dotnet run>'
reproductionSteps.output: docker: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix://$HOME/.docker/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running?

reproductionSteps.description: Run a fallback legacy repro on SkiaSharp 2.88.9 to verify whether XPS creation is available on the current macOS host.
reproductionSteps.command: cd /tmp/repro-3400/legacy-host && dotnet new console -n ReproLegacy --framework net9.0 && cd ReproLegacy && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 2.88.9 && dotnet run && ls -l output.pdf output.xps
reproductionSteps.output: Runtime output: FAIL: SKDocument.CreateXps returned null. output.pdf was created (706 bytes); output.xps size was 0 bytes.

filesCreated.description: Minimal fallback repro that records an SKPicture and attempts PDF/XPS emission on SkiaSharp 2.88.9.
Console project targeting net9.0 with SkiaSharp 2.88.9.

blockers: Issue targets Windows XPS output behavior; current environment is macOS arm64 and cannot execute Windows XPS backend runtime behavior.
On macOS, SKDocument.CreateXps returned null on both 3.119.1 and 2.88.9, preventing PDF-vs-XPS image scaling comparison.
Docker Linux cross-platform attempt could not run because the local Docker daemon was unavailable; Linux also does not validate Windows-only XPS rendering behavior.

corrections.upstream: Triage suggested likely regression reproducible via general repro steps.
corrections.corrected: Reproduction requires a Windows runtime for XPS rendering validation; non-Windows hosts return null from CreateXps and cannot verify image scaling behavior.
